modifications.change_description: modified from Esk et al., 2020

Embryos were grown in 0.5% DMSO from fertilization until prior to dissociation to improve staining in subsequently made gastruloids. Given their much smaller volume due to compaction, gastruloids were regularly spun down between ISH steps to prevent loss of material.

iPSC colonies were not lifted as a whole to form embryoid bodies of varying sizes. Instead iPSCs were dissociated into cell solution and uniformly sized embryoid bodies were prepared using AggreWell Microwell plates.
